diff: The `accounts` parameter for each chain is a list of contracts to be included in Safe Harbor. Each contract listed must include the sub-elements of (1) the `accountAddress` of the contract and (2) the `childContractScope`, which specifies whether child contracts of the specified contract are covered. The possible values for the `childContractScope` parameter are: (1) `None` (no child contracts are in scope), (2) `ExistingOnly` (only child contracts created prior to calling `adoptSafeHarbor` are in scope), or (3) `All` (all child contracts are in scope).
The value of the `scope` parameter is all contracts specified in the Bug Bounty Program.
The `childContractScope` parameter for each contract is specified in the Bug Bounty Program.
description: Add new subdocuments of `A.2.11.1 - Ecosystem Security Infrastructure`. These documents should be located directly after `A.2.11.1.1 - Bug Bounty Program For Critical Infrastructure` and its subdocuments to read as follows: Safe Harbor *(Core)* - Sky Ecosystem is adopting the Security Alliance Safe Harbor Agreement (”Safe Harbor”) by executing the procedures specified in Execution. Safe Harbor is a public agreement that protocols may adopt to waive the right to pursue legal claims against a whitehat hacker, provided that the hacker acts competently, lawfully, and in good faith. Under Safe…

Each contract listed must include the sub-elements of (1) the `accountAddress` of the contract and (2) the `childContractScope`, which specifies whether child contracts of the specified contract are covered. The possible values for the `childContractScope` parameter are: (1) `None` (no child contracts are in scope), (2) `ExistingOnly` (only child contracts created prior to calling `adoptSafeHarbor` are in scope), or (3) `All` (all child contracts are in scope).
The value of the `scope` parameter is all contracts specified in the Bug Bounty Program.
The `childContractScope` parameter for each contract is specified in the Bug Bounty Program.
